constraint in one imperative sentence — directly under the metadata block. See
CONTRIBUTING.md → "ADR conventions". -->
# ADR-0042: Canonical cross-agent skills store with generated per-agent views

**Status:** Accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-14
**Deciders:** @znerol2, umbrella session (claude)
**Rule:** Agent skills have exactly one tracked, agent-neutral source — `.agents/skills/` in the umbrella; every per-agent `…/skills/` view is generated by `bin/sync-skills.sh`, is gitignored, and is never hand-edited — and CI fails on drift.

## Context

Agent skills are **siloed per agent and accrue as stray, unreviewed state**. The umbrella carries ~35 single-file `SKILL.md` skills spread across five locations (`.qwen/skills/` ×27, `.claude/skills/` ×4, a legacy `skills/`, an untracked parent-workspace `.qwen/skills/`, and `dmf-init/.qwen/skills/` ×2), with zero name collisions and no shared index. Agents **auto-author skills mid-task** (`source: auto-skill`), which lands as uncommitted changes that bypass the PR/review gate — a lesson Qwen records is invisible to Claude and Codex, and the working tree is polluted. The real tension is not "where do skills live" but **how to let agents keep auto-capturing knowledge while still routing every durable skill through one reviewed, cross-agent source.** This ADR ratifies the decision half of issue #46; the sync mechanism + CI check are its implementation follow-on.

## Decision

Adopt **one canonical, agent-neutral skills store — `.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` in the umbrella — as the single tracked source of truth**, with every per-agent view generated from it. `.agents/skills/` is the 2026 cross-agent native path (Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and Cursor read it directly, needing no view), which is why it is chosen over `docs/skills/` or a top-level `skills/`.

- **Per-agent views are generated, never authored.** `bin/sync-skills.sh` materializes `.claude/skills/` and `.qwen/skills/` from canonical; both are **gitignored** (no committed duplication). This mirrors the proven `bin/check-working-model-sync.sh` one-source-many-views pattern.
- **Drift is gated.** `bin/sync-skills.sh --check` runs in `.githooks/pre-commit` and as a CI **`skills-sync`** job (modeled on the existing `working-model` job); drift is red.
- **Capture → review → promote.** Agents auto-write to a gitignored `.agents/skills/_inbox/` scratch; a PR promotes vetted skills into canonical. Auto-capture is preserved **without** bypassing review.
- **Sync-by-default, tier by frontmatter.** A skill with no `agents:` field syncs to **all** agents; `agents: [claude]` scopes it to specific agents; `visibility: operator-local` | `experimental` keeps it gitignored (preserving the existing `identity-scrub-sweep` carve-out).
- **Scope.** Umbrella + outliers: absorb the legacy `skills/issues-cruncher` and the parent-workspace `dmf-deploy-commit-workflow`; `dmf-init` keeps its own `.agents/skills/` via the same script with a `--repo` arg; stray component-repo `.qwen/skills/` runtime adds are gitignored (resolving part of #26).

## Consequences

- **Positive** — one tracked copy per skill; auto-minted skills get a review-gated path instead of polluting the tree; knowledge crosses agents; Codex/Gemini/Cursor work with zero glue; drift is CI-enforced, not disciplinary.
- **Negative** — one new script + CI job to own; generated local views rely on `core.symlinks=true` (or a copy fallback); a handful of Claude-runtime-specific skills must be stamped `agents: [claude]` to avoid misleading other agents.
- **Neutral** — `.claude/skills/` and `.qwen/skills/` become gitignored generated artifacts; prose references in `CLAUDE.md`/`QWEN.md` repoint to `.agents/skills/`.

## Alternatives considered

- **`docs/skills/` or top-level `skills/`** (the two locations #46 originally listed) — agent-neutral but **not any agent's native path**, so *every* agent (including Codex/Gemini) needs generated glue. `.agents/skills/` gives the three native-readers for free.
- **Committed per-skill symlinks, no script** — no review gate on auto-minted skills, and whole-dir symlinking `.claude/skills/` collides with Claude's `.system/` internals. Loses the capture→promote gate that is the point of #46.
- **Auto-absorb without an `_inbox`** — moving agent-authored skills straight into canonical bypasses PR review, the exact failure #46 exists to fix.

## Enforcement

`bin/sync-skills.sh --check` (wired into `.githooks/pre-commit` and a CI `skills-sync` job) fails on any drift between canonical and the generated per-agent views; the generated `…/skills/` directories are gitignored so they cannot be hand-edited into `main`. Implementation tracked by issue #46 and specified in `docs/plans/Canonical Cross-Agent Skills 2026-06-14.md`.
